rulings.Case Title: JODIE D. COLLINS SR. et al VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 23, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Fisher Controls International LLC's motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Jodie Collins, Sr. was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive discovery designed to elicit all the evidence they have to support their contention of liability. Defendant's separate statement does not include any facts demonstrating that plaintiffs' responses to defendant's written discovery are factually devoid. The excerpts of decedent's deposition testimony that defendant submitted with its moving papers are not sufficient to demonstrate that decedent did not have knowledge that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. For these reasons, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden by presenting evidence that would require a reasonable trier of fact not to find any underlying material fact more likely than not. While defendant's papers cite to a declaration of Ron Duimstra, such declaration was not provided to the Court nor does it appear that it was filed with defendant's moving papers. The Court will not consider such declaration. (See Uniform Local Rules of Court, Rule 2.7(B).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: WILLI SCHNEIDER ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Defendant D. Zelinsky And Sons, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 23, 2017 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Schneider was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether Mr. Schneider was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether the sheetrock cement to which defendant's employees exposed Mr. Schneider contained asbestos. Plaintiffs' objections to defendant's Exhibits G and M are sustained. Defendant's objections to the July 31, 2015 deposition testimony of Willi Schneider are sustained. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Willi Schneider are overruled. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Charles Ay are sustained as to paragraphs 134-135 as well as to all the statements throughout Mr. Ay's declaration opining that joint compounds contained asbestos through the mid-to-late 1970s, contained in paragraphs 109, 111, 112, 116, 118, 119, 129-132. Defendant's other objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER KORGAN VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel- Civil
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 23, 2017 in Department 503, Line 5. Brayton Purcell LLP, Nancy T. Williams' motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Mark Korgan is granted. Counsel demonstrated she provided Mr. Korgan with proper notice of the motion and no opposition was filed. Hearing required unless counsel informs plaintiff of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/CML)
